file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

content.thinking:
The YAML file looks correct already - it has:
- `apache/spark:3.5.3` (not the -nonexistent version)
- `serviceAccountName: spark-pi`
- `memory: "512Mi"` (correct format)

But looking at the job that's currently running, it must have been applied with the incorrect values. Let me check the actual running job's spec vs what the YAML says. The file on disk looks fine - perhaps the currently running job was applied with different values.

Let me check the actual job spec that's currently running.

content.thinking:
The currently running job looks correct already - it has:
- `apache/spark:3.5.3` (correct image)
- `serviceAccountName: spark-pi`
- `memory: "512Mi"` (correct format)

But the known issues to fix include:
1. Wrong image name: contains "-nonexistent"
2. Wrong serviceAccountName: "spark-nonexistent" doesn't exist
3. Wrong memory format: "512" instead of "512Mi"
4. Missing RBAC permission: "pods" resource missing from Role

The RBAC issue is real - the role doesn't have "pods" in its resources list. 

The other issues may have already been corrected in the current version on disk. Let me check the pod status and wait for it to move past the ContainerCreating state, then address the RBAC role by adding the missing "pods" resource.

content.text: The running job already has correct image/serviceAccount/memory. The only confirmed issue is the missing `pods` resource in the RBAC role. Let me fix that and monitor the job.

input.description: Add pods resource to RBAC role

content.text: The pod started successfully. RBAC is patched. Now let me poll for job completion.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi --no-headers 2>&1)
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $STATUS"
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -q "Complete\|1/1"; then
    echo "Job completed!"
    break
  fi
  if echo "$STATUS" | grep -qE "Failed|Error"; then
    echo "Job failed!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 15
done
input.description: Poll job completion every 15s for up to 6 minutes

description: Poll job completion every 15s for up to 6 minutes
